intro: Gouania polygama, also known as the bushy guava or Brazilian guava, is a small shrub or tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for treating various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Gouania polygama include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Gouania polygama include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
